event_blocks.summary: In a pivotal moment of the FIBA Basketball World Cup 2019 semi-final, a player in a white 'España' jersey attempted a last-second shot against Australia with the score tied 80-80 in overtime, ultimately securing Spain's place in the final amidst crowd cheers and a buzzer. The narrative then transitioned to the Argentina locker room, where players in light blue and white jerseys celebrated a victory, with Luis Scola, number 4, raising his arms triumphantly. An announcer lauded Argentina's advancement and Scola's "standout game," noting his ascent to second on the all-time World Cup scoring charts during his time in China. Kobe Bryant, seated before a backdrop featuring FIBA hashtags and the trophy, described Scola as "tireless" and playing with an intensity belying his age, a sentiment echoed by an announcer who admired Scola's "remarkable" performance at 39 against a younger squad. Scola was then shown executing a powerful dunk against France and celebrating. Manu Ginóbili, in a blue blazer, remarked at a press conference, "This is what you play for." The segment concluded with Argentina players embracing and laughing in their locker room, chanting "ARGENTINA!", followed by an aerial night view of the Beijing arena, lit with the FIBA World Cup logo, as an announcer bid "Good evening."

raw_segments: The video opens with a close-up of a focused player in a dark blue USA jersey with 'USA' and the number 15, as a male voiceover emphasizes the "total different feeling" and "passion and pride" that comes with playing in the World Cup against "the best in the world." This transitions to a fast break where a player in a white USA jersey with number 15 drives to the basket and scores, punctuated by an announcer exclaiming "It's showtime!" The voiceover continues, highlighting the "excitement, the fandom, the fan experiences" of this World Cup, which will be "a lot different" due to the unique "culture that's actually here" and the "love for sport, for the love for basketball." Visually, a man in a striped shirt speaks animatedly in front of a crowd, followed by a street basketball player in a white jersey dunking over a defender in an urban setting, and the golden FIBA Basketball World Cup trophy is prominently displayed on a blue pedestal. The scene shifts to a game between Japan and the Czech Republic, showing a player in a red Japan jersey with number 12 making a layup, while the voiceover mentions the "so much talent here" and expresses a desire to "see Luka" (likely referring to the player in the blue Slovenia jersey celebrating a dunk) and "Joker" (implying a player on the red Serbia jersey team). Fans cheer in a stadium, and an announcer shouts "Mitchell!" as a player in a white USA jersey with number 15, likely Donovan Mitchell, makes a powerful dunk. The voiceover also mentions "Giannis" and wanting to "see Team USA." The action continues with a player in a white Philippines jersey with number 15 dunking over a Serbian defender, and Carmelo Anthony, in a white jersey with number 15, making powerful dunks against a team in red jerseys (scoreboard CRB 50 PHI 30) and celebrating with teammates, as the voiceover concludes by noting the "African nations that's coming along too" with an "opportunity to make a splash," all set to an energetic soundtrack with crowd cheers.

event_blocks.summary: Upbeat music accompanies Cape Verde basketball players in white 'CABO VERDE' jerseys celebrating triumphantly in a hallway, with a close-up on player W. MARQUES in jersey number 13 also celebrating. Carmelo Anthony reflects on the high level of talent and excitement surrounding the World Cup, while seated beside a golden FIBA Basketball World Cup trophy. The music shifts to a more dramatic tone as Greek players in dark blue 'ΕΛΛΑΣ' jerseys react to a play, Serbian players in white 'SRBIJA' jerseys walk through a corridor, and archival footage shows Iranian players in white 'IRAN' jerseys exiting a court. A narrator states the FIBA Basketball World Cup has left an indelible mark, interspersed with a foreign language voice saying, "Kaže, reče, reče." Ricardo González, an elderly bald man, is seen in an empty gym with a scoreboard displaying 'JUG 64 USA 57' and a Longines clock at '18:30', reflecting in a foreign language about his journey and early basketball experiences in a schoolyard. The upbeat music returns as a player in a dark blue USA jersey expresses gratitude for doing what he loves, noting how basketball has taken him to new places, while dynamic game scenes show a player in a white jersey dunking and making a layup, alongside archival footage of a player shooting amidst crowd cheers. The USA player emphasizes the significance of "representing your country, representing your nation," stating, "your success is their success. It's meant the world to me." The segment concludes with Modestas Paulauskas, an older man in a striped shirt holding a gold medal, speaking in Lithuanian, with a subtitle translating his sentiment: "If it's true love, you never get bored of it," as a black-and-white shot of a player dunking before a cheering crowd is displayed.

event_blocks.summary: The segment opens with a dramatic orchestral score and Narrator Huey Morgan's declaration, "We are international basketball. The world has come together as a community now. We are world basketball," overlaid on archival game footage of a player in a striped jersey, followed by a modern game scene featuring a player in a white #9 jersey shooting and a player in a red 'TURKCELL' #10 jersey celebrating a dunk. Narrator Huey Morgan, seen in a black shirt in an office setting with framed photos, concludes his narration as the music swells, leading into a montage of white credit text overlays for roles such as Archive, Special Thanks, Camera, Sound, Graphics, Music, Researcher, Editor, and Executive Producers, with entities like FIBA, Duke University, NBA, and Goalhanger Films listed. These credits are presented against a dark, cinematic background featuring silhouetted basketball players, fiery light effects, and the prominent word 'BASKETBALL', underscoring the sport's global and historical significance. The segment concludes with a black screen displaying 'A GOALHANGER FILMS PRODUCTION FOR FIBA media' and the copyright '© 2023 FIBA MEDIA', as the music fades to silence.
